name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram operates a global network of agent locations offering money transfers, check cashing, and bill payment services through retail partners and standalone locations.
description_long:
MoneyGram is an established money transfer and financial services company that operates through a distributed network of agent locations rather than company-owned branches. The company leverages partnerships with retail businesses, convenience stores, and specialized service providers to offer its services to consumers. According to their Seattle location page, they maintain multiple agent locations across the city, including partnerships with CVS pharmacies, wireless retailers, and specialty shops.

MoneyGram's core services include domestic and international money transfers, check cashing, money order issuance, and bill payment capabilities. Customers can initiate transactions online or visit physical agent locations to complete transfers and financial services. The platform emphasizes convenience through both digital and in-person channels, allowing customers to manage transfers flexibly based on their preference.

The MoneyGram network model distinguishes it from traditional banking by focusing on accessibility through widely distributed retail partnerships. Rather than operating dedicated branches, they embed services into existing retail locations, which increases geographic reach and convenience for consumers in various neighborhoods. This agent-based model has enabled extensive local availability as evidenced by seven search results for Seattle alone.

MoneyGram serves consumers who need quick access to money transfer and check-cashing services without traditional banking relationships. However, consumers should recognize that specific fees, exchange rates, transfer limits, and service terms are not disclosed on their location finder page, requiring direct contact with agent locations for pricing details. The reliance on third-party agent locations means service quality and hours may vary by location.
address: 13754 Aurora Ave N b, Seattle, WA 98133

company_info.headquarters: 13754 Aurora Ave N b, Seattle, WA 98133

pros: Extensive network of agent locations across Seattle (7 locations found in single city search)
Multiple service channels: online transfers and in-person agent locations for flexibility
Partnership with major retailers like CVS provides convenient neighborhood access
Services available through specialized retailers (wireless shops, grocery stores, restaurants) beyond traditional financial institutions
Both domestic and international money transfer capabilities mentioned on platform
Weekend and early morning availability at select locations (some open 6:00 AM)
Established global brand with decades of money transfer experience
cons: No fee, exchange rate, or pricing information disclosed on location finder—requires contacting agents directly
Service availability and quality depend on individual agent locations, creating inconsistency
Several locations show limited hours (CVS #10331 opens at 12:00 AM, unclear if midnight or noon)
No online pricing comparison tool or rate calculator available on this page
Agent-based model means services are not available 24/7 at most locations
best_for: Consumers needing quick money transfers to domestic or international recipients
Individuals without traditional bank accounts seeking check-cashing services
Customers who prefer in-person transactions over digital-only platforms
People in neighborhoods with CVS or retail agent locations nearby
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for consumers who need accessible money transfer and check-cashing services through convenient retail locations rather than traditional banks. The primary caveat is that specific pricing, fees, and terms are unavailable on their website—customers must contact individual agent locations for quotes, and service quality may vary significantly based on the specific agent location used.
